title: oxalis suteroides
intro: Oxalis suteroides, also known as the New Zealand sorrel or wood sorrel, is a perennial plant species in the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to lower blood pressure and reduce inflammation due to its high levels of oxalic acid. The health benefits of Oxalis suteroides include its potential to treat urinary tract infections, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Oxalis suteroides can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves, which are often used to treat various health conditions, and it can also be eaten fresh in salads for its tangy, sour flavor.
